finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_13.md:217; chapters/chapter_13.md:219; chapters/chapter_13.md:221; chapters/chapter_13.md:223; chapters/chapter_13.md:225; chapters/chapter_13.md:227
finding_results.notes: Replaced Dara's composed mini-essay ('And now it doesn't do that. It just does this. I don't know how to separate those.') with shorter, concrete, performance-keyed turns. Dara now false-starts at line 217 ('And it was --' into 'Your times were there. When it was doing that.'), gets Lena's claim at 219 ('It wasn't me.'), circles back to the concrete number at 221 ('Five seconds, though. At easy.'), receives Lena's acknowledgment at 223, and shows being stuck physically at 225 (pulling at the elastic) before her flat closing at 227. No single Dara line packages slower times, reduced senses, the quiet, and the challenge together. Dara's incomprehension emerges through repetition of the five-second gap and inability to frame the abstract distinction Lena is drawing, consistent with her flat, process-oriented, literal characterization. Lena's responses remain direct and slightly formal per her moderate-contraction, second-language register.

summary: Single finding addressed. Dara's post-race exchange with Lena was revised from a composed philosophical observation into shorter turns anchored to concrete performance facts. The previous version had Dara articulating the impasse neatly ('I don't know how to separate those'); the revision has her repeating the five-second gap and getting stuck when Lena's answer operates on a register she cannot access. Dara's false start, number-repetition, and physical gesture of being stuck replace the thesis-speech quality while preserving the scene's core divergence between the two characters. All other chapter dialogue was reviewed and found consistent with style bible voice profiles and contraction levels.
